name: The Family & Youth Institute
mission: The FYI strengthens and empowers individuals, families, and communities through research and education efforts that promote positive youth development, ...
theoryOfChange: The Family & Youth Institute (thefyi.org) explicitly mentions 'the fyi's theory of change' on their Research page. They describe their approach as a community-based participatory research model that ensures projects are grounded in community spirit and collaboration. This involves partnering with communities to identify priorities and needs, engaging and integrating community voices throughout the research process, incorporating community perspectives on data, identifying practical solutions, and sharing the benefits. This outlines how their activities lead to impact.

all_citations.claim: The FYI focuses on research and education for youth development and maintains strong financial metadata.
The organization reported total revenue of $850,837, a program expense ratio of 83.6%, 23.6 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.09 per $1 raised.
The organization provides various donation options to support its community workshops and research efforts.
The Family and Youth Institute is Zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy for its programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The FYI earns a score of 64/100, reflecting strong financial health and efficient operations balanced by a need for more direct impact data. The organization maintains a high program expense ratio and significant working capital reserves.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact evaluations to verify long-term behavioral outcomes
Moderate data confidence levels regarding external verification of program directness

dimension_explanations.alignment: The FYI shows a medium donor fit with a score of 28/50, specifically catering to those interested in mental health advocacy and community development [1]. The organization is Zakat-eligible, accepting funds to support its various community-facing programs [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high operational capacity with a program expense ratio of 83.6% and 23.6 months of working capital [1][2]. While its capacity-building model through research and education is robust, the impact score of 36/50 reflects a focus on indirect service delivery rather than direct cost-per-beneficiary metrics [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Family and Youth Institute (The FYI) empowers individuals and families through research-backed education and community-based mental health initiatives.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 83.6% ensures the majority of funds directly support research and education
Strong financial stability with 23.6 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of only $0.09 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: The Family and Youth Institute is a research and education organization dedicated to promoting positive youth development and strengthening family structures [1]. In 2023, the organization reported a total revenue of $850,837 to support its mission of providing community workshops and evidence-based resources [2].

rich_narrative.headline: The Family and Youth Institute (The FYI) empowers individuals and families through research-backed education and community-based mental health initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value research-driven community development and want to support the long-term mental health infrastructure of the American Muslim community.
donor_motivations: Want to fund evidence-based resources for Muslim families
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Prefer supporting intellectual and educational capacity building
Seek to fulfill zakat through specialized research initiatives
giving_considerations: The organization focuses on long-term social change rather than immediate emergency relief
Financial reserves are unusually high, suggesting a very conservative fiscal approach
Impact is measured through educational reach rather than individual case management
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking direct humanitarian aid (food/medicine) or those who require rigorous RCT-level evidence of behavioral change.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a strong theoretical framework and verified outcomes, but lacks independent third-party evaluations or cost-per-beneficiary data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The FYI uses research to create educational workshops and resources that empower community leaders and families, leading to improved mental health outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that community workshops and resources actually lead to the intended positive developmental outcomes for youth.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a total revenue under $1M and a broad national mission, the organization has significant capacity to scale its research and workshop delivery.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an exceptionally high <cite id="6">working capital ratio of 23.6 months</cite> compared to peers.

similar_organizations.differentiator: More focus on community healing and activism

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="4">83.6% of its total expenses</cite> toward its research and educational programs, exceeding industry benchmarks for lean operations.
With <cite id="6">23.6 months of working capital</cite> available, the charity possesses the financial stability to sustain operations through economic fluctuations.
The FYI operates with high efficiency, requiring only <cite id="5">$0.09 in fundraising costs</cite> for every dollar raised.

strengths_deep_dive: The FYI's operational model is built on a foundation of high fiscal responsibility and strategic resource allocation. By maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 83.6%</cite>, the organization ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are converted into tangible community resources and research outputs. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 6.1%</cite>, indicating steady growth and a sustainable funding trajectory. Their financial health is further evidenced by a <cite id="6">working capital ratio of 23.6 months</cite>, which is significantly higher than the standard 6-12 month recommendation for non-profits, allowing them to invest in long-term research projects without immediate funding pressure.
rich_narrative.summary: The Family and Youth Institute (The FYI) serves as a specialized research and education hub dedicated to <cite id="1">strengthening and empowering individuals, families, and communities</cite> through evidence-based mental health and developmental initiatives. Operating primarily within the United States, the organization addresses the unique psychological and social needs of Muslim communities by bridging the gap between academic research and community practice. In the 2023 tax year, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $850,837</cite>, which fuels its core activities in <cite id="3">research, education, community workshops, and resource creation</cite>. The FYI distinguishes itself by maintaining a lean operational model, ensuring that a significant portion of its budget is dedicated to its mission-driven programs. Financial data indicates a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 83.6%</cite>, reflecting a strong commitment to direct service delivery over administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="5">$0.09 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. This financial discipline is complemented by a robust liquidity position, as the charity maintains <cite id="6">23.6 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a significant buffer for long-term strategic planning and organizational stability.
